assessment_items.question_content: ** *The Mask of Agamemnon* is one of many Mycenaean objects, which was made of  [[☃ dropdown 1]]  and found in  [[☃ dropdown 2]] .**
hints:
Heinrich Schliemann discovered about 30 pounds of gold objects in multiple shaft tombs in Grave Circles A and B at Mycenae.  

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/ancient-art-civilizations/aegean-art1/mycenaean/v/agamemnon-mask)**
Predating Greek civilization, Mycenaean and other Ancient Aegean cultures were the basis of Greek mythology.

Agamemnon was a legendary king in Greek mythology.  

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/ancient-art-civilizations/aegean-art1/mycenaean/v/agamemnon-mask)**

choices.content: a legendary hero in Greek mythology.

an amateur archaeologist who discovered this mask:
![Mask of Agamemnon, from shaft grave V, grave circle A, c. 1550–1500 B.C.E. (Mycenae), gold, 35 cm high (National Archaeological Museum, Athens)](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/85c8074bb55d4c7ee98abcdd6b0a249c.jpg)
a Mycenaean ruler buried in Grave Circle A.

assessment_items.question_content:
** Which technique was used to produce the *Mask of Agamemnon*? **

![Mask of Agamemnon, from shaft grave V, grave circle A, c. 1550–1500 B.C.E. (Mycenae), gold, 35 cm high (National Archaeological Museum, Athens)](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6993c9202c1231ec11f017cfcea3886a.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Carving is a subtractive technique used to take away material such as wood or stone in order to produce a final sculpture.
Casting refers to a technique where molten metal or other liquid media is poured into a mold, and which is later removed.

Mycenaean metalworkers hammered gold sheets over wooden forms to create the sculpture.

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/ancient-art-civilizations/aegean-art1/mycenaean/v/agamemnon-mask)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**What leads some scholars to question the authenticity of the *Mask of Agamemnon*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Although the gold objects Schliemann discovered in Mycenaean Grave Circles are authentic, this mask differs in appearance from other masks from the site, leading some to suggest Schliemann restored it to appeal to 19th century tastes.

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/ancient-art-civilizations/aegean-art1/mycenaean/v/agamemnon-mask)**

choices.content: Scholars have proven other gold objects found at the site to be fake.
The facial features appear heavily restored and differ from similar masks found at the site.
No evidence exists to prove this mask belonged to Agamemnon.
Mycenaean culture lacked the technical capacity to create such skillful forms.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Based on the information in this video, which statements are TRUE of Mycenaean culture?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The fortified buildings and weapons characteristic of Mycenaean culture has led many scholars to describe these people as militaristic.
Mycenaean culture flourished during the Bronze Age, characterized by the production and use of bronze tools.

Mycenaean tombs and objects found at these burial sites provide one of the primary sources of information about the culture. Written texts are not a primary source of information about Mycenaean culture.

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/ancient-art-civilizations/aegean-art1/mycenaean/v/agamemnon-mask)**

choices.content: Many scholars believe Mycenae was more militaristic than other Aegean cultures of the period.
Objects found at burial sites are a primary source of information about this ancient culture.
Mycenaean culture possessed advanced metalworking techniques.
Written texts are a primary source of information about this ancient culture.
